name: rest_sets_0_reps_0
instructions: Rest day - take it easy!

name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on a bench that has a vertical backrest while in an upright position, holding the bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder-width just above the level of your shoulders. Rotate your wrists to unhook the bar. Exhale and press the bar overhead, extending your arms while keeping your head faced forward.

name: Reverse Snow Angels
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with arms at your sides, palms facing up. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Slowly lift your chest and arms off the ground, keeping your legs down. Move your arms out to the sides, forming a T-shape with your body. Continue the motion, bringing your arms overhead to form a Y-shape. As you move your arms, rotate your shoulders so your palms face down at the top of the movement.Reverse the movement, bringing your arms back down to your sides.Lower your chest and arms to the starting position.

name: Close Grip EZ bar Curl
instructions: Stand straight and be comfortable. Place your feet shoulder-width apart, with your knees slightly bent. Keep your torso straight and tight, and grab the EZ bar using a close underhand grip with your arms extended. Raise the EZ bar towards your chest while keeping your elbows perfectly straight.

name: EZ Bar Curl
instructions: Keeping your eyes facing forward, elbows tucked in at your sides, and your body completely still, slowly curl the bar up. Squeeze your biceps hard at the top of the movement, and then slowly lower it back to the starting position. The bar should not be touching your body.

name: Cable Flies
instructions: Set the cables at chest height then face away from the machine and grip both handles, step forward into a staggered stance. Squeeze the chest muscles and press your arms forward until the handles meet back in front of the chest

name: Seated Tricep Extension
instructions: Position an adjustable bench at 90 degrees. To get into position, sit in an upright position and lift the dumbbell to the top of your shoulder. Take a deep breath, overlap your hands around the dumbbell, then press it into position overhead. Maintain an overlapping grip and slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by unlocking your elbows.Once your forearms reach parallel or just below, drive the dumbbell back to the starting point by extending the elbows and flexing the triceps.
muscleGroups: Long Head Triceps;Lateral Head Triceps;Medial Head Triceps

name: Back Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie on a bench with a dumbbell over your chest, elbows slightly bent. Lower the weight behind your head while keeping body flat and feel your upper back muscles stretching, then pull back to start.

name: Chest Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Extend your arms toward the ceiling, over your chest. Your palms should face each other, and your elbows should be slightly bent. Inhale and extend the weights back and over your head, keeping a strong back and core. take about 3 to 4 seconds to reach a fully extended position where the dumbbell is parallel to your head. Exhale slowly and return your arms to the starting position

name: Smith Machine Calve Raises
instructions: Find a weight plate that is large enough for you to step on. A 45 lb plate is perfect. If you have a sturdy step-up, then use that. If you don’t trust your step, or think that your step is too light for this lift, then a weight plate is your safest option.Place the weight plate in the center of the barbell, on the interior part of the Smith Machine. The edge of the plate should meet the barbell. You want the weight plate to be in front of the barbell but also centered in the middle of the barbell.Load your barbell up with your desired weight. If you leave the barbell weightless or too light, then the Smith Machine calf raise can backfire on you. If when you start this lift, it feels bouncy and disengaging, or the barbell is bouncing off of your back, then you probably need to add more weight.Place the barbell on your back as if you were doing a barbell back squat, and step up with the top 1/3 of your foot on the plate. Once you are in the starting position and feel like you can comfortably push the barbell up with the balls of your feet, you are ready to begin your set.With a straight leg, push off with the balls of your feet, and unlatch the barbell from the Smith Machine by gripping the bar and rotating your wrists slightly backward.Let your heel drop down until it hits the floor. Push back up with the balls of your feet.Squeeze your upper calf on the way up to facilitate proper gastrocnemius engagement.

name: Skullcrushers
instructions: Your upper arm should be just past 90 degrees, at a 91 or 92 degree angle. Keep your wrists strong and a tight grip on the bar. Lower the weight slowly down to an inch above your forehead, moving only at the elbows. Don't allow your shoulders to shift forward; keep your upper arms still.

name: Powerlifting Squat
instructions: Place the barbell in a high or low bar position below the prominent bone at the base of your neck (the seventh cervical vertebra). Ensure the bar rests evenly across your upper back and traps. Keep your chest up and shoulders back to create a solid shelf for the bar to rest on. Set your squat stance with feet in a slightly wider stance than shoulder-width point toes outward at approximately 15-30 degrees, Ensure both feet are evenly spaced from center. Align knees to track in the same direction as toes, keep knees stable (avoid letting them cave inward). Pull shoulder blades back and down, keep chest lifted, stack ribs over hips and maintain the natural curve in the lower back (neutral spine). Take a deep breath into your belly, brace abdominal and back muscles like preparing for a punch and maintain this tension throughout the movement and begin to squat

name: Cardio
instructions: Variation of cardio, such as running, cycling, swimming, rowing, elliptical, etc.

name: Overhead Rope Extension
instructions: Attach a rope to the bottom pulley of a pulley machine.Step 2: Hold onto the rope with both hands and extend your arms with your hands directly over your head with your palms facing each other. Hold your elbows in close to your head and keep your arms perpendicular to the ceiling with your knuckles pointed toward the ceiling. This is the starting position.Step 3: Lower the rope behind your head slowly as you hold your upper arms still. Inhale as you do so and pause when your triceps are fully extended.Step 4: Go back to the starting position by flexing your triceps. Exhale as you do so.

name: Seated Shoulder Press
instructions: Set up an adjustable angle bench to 90 degrees and select the desired weight from the rack.Pick up the dumbbells from the floor using a neutral grip (palms facing in). Position the end of the dumbbells on your knees and sit down on the bench.Using a safe and controlled motion, kick your knees up one at a time in order to get each dumbbell into place.Once the dumbbells are in place, rotate your palms so they are facing forward.Take a deep breath then press the dumbbells overhead by extending the elbows and contracting the deltoids.Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position (the arms should be roughly 90 degrees or slightly lower depending upon limb lengths).

name: Close Grip Lat Pull Down
instructions: Set seat at a comfortable height. Grab the handle, using a neutral grip if it's a V-handle or underhand grip for the more standard wide bar. Focus on keeping your feet planted into the ground, core tight, and maintain a tight grip on the bar. Lean back slightly—do not arch your back for this—then, squeezing your shoulder blades, drive them down as you pull. Remember to drive your elbows down as well as you pull the bar to the top of your chest. Hold momentarily at the bottom, then return to start position under control.

name: Tricep Bench Dips
instructions: Position you self just off the edge of the bench with hands shoulder width apart on the edge in good posture. knees ar 90 degrees. Slowly lower your body by bending the elbows and shoulders until your arm at the forearms creates a 90 degree angle. Maintain posture throughout

name: Face Pull
instructions: Reach up and grasp the handles with both hands with your palms facing in. Step back until your arms are fully extended, then engage your core and lean back slightly, positioning your body at a roughly 20-degree angle. Pull the rope toward you just enough to start lifting the weight from the stack, then engage your shoulders, rolling them back to create good posture—you don't want your shoulders hunching or rolling forward. This is your starting position. Pull the handles of the attachment straight toward your forehead. Keep your palms facing in as your elbows flare outward toward the sides, engaging the rear delts.

name: Chin Ups
instructions: Using a supinated grip, grasp the bar with a shoulder width grip. Take a deep breath, squeeze your glutes and brace your abs. Depress the shoulder blades and then drive the elbows straight down to the floor while activating the lats. Pull your chin towards the bar until the lats are fully contracted and the chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back to the start position

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Grip a dumbbell in your left hand, then place your right knee and right hand on the left edge of a bench. ...Tuck in your ribs to create a neutral spine and engage your core. Press your right hand firmly into the bench to create a strong base and then row the dumbbell towards your waist.

name: Single Arm Overhead Tricep Dumbbell Extension
instructions: Sit with your feet and glutes firmly planted and one arm flexed behind your head holding a dumbbell .With your other hand on your hip exhale and extend the dumbbell overhead, focusing on keeping your upper arm from moving. Continue until your arm is straight and the dumbbell is overhead.Inhale and allow your arm to bend, returning to the starting position. Do not lock your elbow

name: Barbell Overhead Press
instructions: Stand with the bar on your front shoulders, and your hands next to your shoulders.Press the bar over your head, until it's balanced over your shoulders and mid-foot.Lock your elbows at the top, and shrug your shoulders to the ceiling.

name: Donkey Calf Raise Machine
instructions: step on the foot plate, hinge at the waist, and situate the upper pad on your lower back. From this position, contract your calves to lift up on your toes. Squeeze your calves hard at the top and slowly return to the starting position.

name: Cable Single Arm Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Stand at a cable station with handle attachment. Elbow pinned to side, push rope downward until arm fully extends. Return rope to chest height under control without letting your elbow move

name: Incline Bicep Curl
instructions: Sit against an incline bench (55–65° degree). With supinated grip, let arms fully extend behind the body. Curl dumbbells to shoulders, emphasizing the long head of the biceps, then lower slowly.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Grasp the barbell with an underhand grip, shoulder-width apart. Bend your elbows and curl the barbell up towards your chest, squeezing your biceps at the top. Slowly lower the barbell back to starting position, keeping tension on the biceps muscles throughout.

name: Cable Chest Press
instructions: Hold a handle in each hand in an overhand grip, then stand between the cables with your back turned towards the equipment. Position your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and take a step forward. Your front knee should be slightly bent.Lift your arms and bend the elbows 90 degrees. Your elbows and forearms should be in line with the resistance line. This is your starting position.Take a deep breath and hold it for a second. Engage your core to keep your spine stable.On the exhale, press your arms forward. Your thumbs should connect at the center – this is the finishing position.Inhale as you return your arms to the starting position. All movements should be done slowly and with as much control as possible.

name: Good Morning Dumbbells
instructions: Stand with a dumbbell across your chest, knees slightly bent. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight. Feel tension in hamstrings, then drive hips forward to stand.

name: Lying Tricep Extension
instructions: Lie back on a bench, placing your head near the end of the bench. Squeeze your glutes and focus on driving your feet into the ground. Begin to drop the dumbbell behind your head, driving your elbows toward the ceiling. Avoid flaring your elbows—keep your arms tight and close to your torso

name: Seated Leg Curl Machine
instructions: Select the desired weight, then adjust the pad so it rests comfortably above the back of the heels.Tense up the hamstrings by taking the weight slightly off the stack. This is the starting position for the exercise.Take a deep breath, squeeze the hamstrings, and curl the weight up as far as possible while keeping the spine neutral.Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position and repeat.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Start in a supine position with your back on a bench and roll a barbell up to the crease of your hips. Drive both feet into the floor and squeeze your glutes while bridging your hips up. Lower your hips back to the starting position.

name: Deadlift
instructions: Grasp the bar just outside the line of your knees with an overhand or mixed grip.Position your feet shoulder-width apart (or not much more) with toes under the bar. Your feet point straight ahead, or they can angle out slightly. Heels should stay flat on the surface. As you lift and lower the bar, it will travel close to the shins and may even graze them. Maintain a neutral spine position.Engage your abdominal muscles.Slowly bring the weight down as far as you can while maintaining proper form, softening your knees, and keeping your back flat.

name: Towel Bicep Curls
instructions: Start in a sitting position. Bend your right knee and place the middle of the towel underneath your right leg. With your arms by your side and palms facing forward holding the towel, pull on the towel and bend your arms until your arms are flexed. For resistance, press down on the towel with your right leg. Lower your arms back down until your arms are back by your side.

name: Arm Circles
instructions: Stand tall, arms extended out at shoulder level. Make small forward circles for desired reps, then reverse. Warms up shoulders and improves mobility.

name: Machine Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit on the machine with your upper arms resting on the pad, (adjust the machine so you can comfortably rest your arms on the pad) your hands should grip the handles with an underhand grip. Exhale and curl the handles toward your shoulder while keeping your elbows firmly pressed into the pad.Continue to curl until you reach about shoulder level.Inhale and lower the handles, returning to the starting position.

name: Band Reverse Pulls
instructions: Hold a resistance band at eye level, hands slightly wider than shoulders. Pull the band outward, squeezing rear delts and upper back. Control the return. Good for posture.

name: Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench, grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Lower the bar to mid-chest, then press up powerfully. Keep shoulders retracted, feet firm on the ground.

name: Bicep Dumbbell Curls
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, palms forward. Curl dumbbells toward your shoulders, keeping elbows tight to torso. Lower under control.

name: Bicep Push Ups
instructions: A close-stance push-up variation that shifts emphasis to biceps by keeping elbows snug to the torso and palms turned slightly outward. Maintain a rigid core throughout.

name: Bodyweight Squats
instructions: Feet shoulder-width, toes out slightly. Brace core, drop hips back and down until thighs near parallel. Drive up through heels. Keep knees out and back neutral.

name: Dynamic Stretching
instructions: Perform light dynamic stretches for 5 minutes.

name: Cable Reverse Fly
instructions: Stand between two cable stacks set at chest height. Grip opposite handles with arms slightly forward. Pull arms out and back, targeting rear delts and upper back.

name: Calf Raises
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells (or bodyweight) for balance. Raise heels upward, pause, then lower under control. Focus on a steady, controlled tempo.

name: Chest Supported Dumbbell Row
instructions: Set an incline bench at ~45°. Lie chest-down, arms hanging with dumbbells. Pull elbows back to row, squeezing upper back. Lower slowly.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with a barbell. Grip the bar with your hands closer than shoulder-width apart. Lower the bar to your chest and then press upward, focusing on your triceps.

name: Close Grip Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie flat holding dumbbells at chest level, palms facing each other. Press up, keeping dumbbells close together to target triceps and inner chest. Lower slowly.

name: Close Grip Push Ups
instructions: Assume a push-up position with hands slightly narrower than shoulders. Keep elbows close to ribs as you lower and push back up, emphasizing triceps and chest.

name: Ab Routine
instructions: 5 abdominal Variations

name: Crunches
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent. Hands gently behind ears or crossed on chest. Lift shoulder blades off the floor by contracting abs, then lower. Avoid pulling on your neck.

name: Lower Back Extension
instructions: Place your thighs on the pad. Bend your knees slightly and secure your feet, keeping them in line with your knees. Extend your arms toward the floor.Exhale and move up until your shoulders, spine, and hips are in line. Engage your core and gently slide your shoulders back.Inhale and bend down from your waist. Touch the floor.

name: Static Stretching
instructions: Perform static stretches for 5 minutes.

name: 12line Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a 12line bench with a barbell. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower it to your lower chest, then press upward.

name: Diamond Push ups
instructions: Form a triangle/diamond with your hands beneath your chest. Lower yourself by bending elbows, then push back up, targeting triceps and inner chest.

name: Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding dumbbells at chest level. Press them upward, keeping elbows at ~45°. Lower slowly, focusing on chest contraction.

name: Dumbbell Deadlift
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, knees slightly bent. Hinge at hips, lowering weights near shins. Push through heels, keeping back flat, to stand upright. Squeeze glutes at top.

name: Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Pick up the dumbbells off the floor using a neutral grip (palms facing in). Position the ends of the dumbbells in your hip crease, and sit down on the bench.To get into position, lay back and keep the weights close to your chest. Once you are in position, take a deep breath, then press the dumbbells to lockout at the top.Slightly retract your shoulder blades, unlock your elbows, and slowly lower the dumbbells laterally while maintaining the angle at your elbow.Once the dumbbells reach chest level, reverse the movement by squeezing your pecs together and bringing the dumbbells back to their starting position.

name: Dumbbell Shrugs
instructions: Kneel torso slightly forward while holding the dumbbells at your sides with a neutral grip, shrug shoulders toward ears, pause, then lower slowly. Keep neck relaxed, focusing on upper traps.

name: dumbell chest
instructions: push

name: Dumbbell Reverse Snow Angels
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with arms at your sides holding the dumbbells, palms facing up. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Slowly lift your chest and arms off the ground, keeping your legs down. Move your arms out to the sides, forming a T-shape with your body.Continue the motion, bringing your arms overhead to form a Y-shape. As you move your arms, rotate your shoulders so your palms face down at the top of the movement.Reverse the movement, bringing your arms back down to your sides.Lower your chest and arms to the starting position.

name: Flat Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with dumbbells at chest level. Press the weights upward until your arms are fully extended, then lower them slowly.

name: Front Dumbbell Raise
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides. Lift the weights in front of you to shoulder height with a slight bend in the elbows, then lower slowly.

name: Front Barbell Raises
instructions: Stand with in in front of thighs, palms in. Lift arms forward to shoulder height, focusing on front delts. Lower slowly, avoiding back sway.

name: Glute Bridge Dumbbell
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place a dumbbell across your hips if desired. Squeeze glutes to lift hips into a straight line from shoulders to knees, then lower slowly.

name: Superman Hold Dumbbells
instructions: Lie facedown on a flat bench, with your chest reaching the end of the bench with arms hanging down.Hold a light weight in each hand with palms facing towards one another.Stretch your arms directly in front of you so that they create a straight line with your torso. Raise your arms as far as they can go, while keeping them straight. Hold this position. Lower your arms back down and then repeat.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest level with both hands. Stand with feet shoulder-width. Squat down, keeping chest up and knees tracking over toes. Push through heels to stand.

name: Good Morning
instructions: Stand with a dumbbell across your chest, knees slightly bent. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight. Feel tension in hamstrings, then drive hips forward to stand.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells with your palms facing inward. Curl the weights upward while keeping your wrists neutral, then lower slowly.

name: Hammer Curls
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells, palms facing inward. Curl up without rotating wrists, emphasizing brachialis and forearms. Lower slowly.

name: Hamstring Curl Machine
instructions: While grasping the machine's handles, push down on the lower leg pad, pulling it as close to your glutes as you can. Hold for 2 seconds, then return to the starting position

name: Hamstring Curls
instructions: Lie face down, optionally place a light dumbbell between your feet. Curl heels toward your butt, squeezing your hamstrings. Lower slowly.

name: Incline Barbell Press
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with feet planted. Grip the bar slightly wider than your shoulders. Lower it toward your upper chest, then press upward, focusing on your upper chest and shoulders.

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with your arms hanging straight. Curl the dumbbells upward while keeping your elbows fixed, then lower slowly.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie on an incline bench (30–45°angle) hands positioned at your shoulders, elbows bent and angled down below your ribs. Relax your neck against the bench, Keep your feet flat on the floor. Brace your core and press both dumbbells straight over your chest as you exhale. Keep your wrists straight and at the top of the movement the dumbbells should be very close to eachother (without touching). Slowly lower the dumbbells to the top of your chest as you inhale. As you lower the dumbbells, your elbows should come down at roughly a 45-degree angle to your torso, always keep your elbows pointing to the floor on the way down.

name: Incline Press
instructions: Lie back on an incline bench with dumbbells at shoulder height. Press them upward, focusing on upper chest and front delts. Lower under control.

name: Incline Smith Machine Bench Press
instructions: Set the bench at a 90 degree angle and push the bar without locking your elbows

name: Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand. Arms are at your sides, palms facing in. Position your feet roughly hip-distance apart. Check your posture—roll your shoulders back, engage your coge, and look straight ahead.Raise your arms simultaneously just a couple of inches out to each side and pause. This momentary pause should help ensure you disengage your trapezius muscle from the movement, targeting the deltoids as intended.Lift the dumbbells up and out to each side, keeping your arms almost completely straight, stopping when your elbows reach shoulder height and your body forms a "T" shape. Breathe in as you lift.Pause and hold for a second at the top of the movement.Lower the weights slowly (take about twice as long to lower the weights as you took to lift them), bringing your arms back to your sides. Exhale as you lower the dumbbells.

name: Lateral Raises
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at sides, palms in. Raise arms out to shoulder height, focusing on side delts. Pause, then lower slowly.

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Place your hands on the hand bars. Lift the weight while exhaling until your legs are almost straight. Do not lock your knees. Keep your back against the backrest, and do not arch your back. Exhale and lower the weight back to the starting position.

name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine with feet shoulder-width on the platform. Press through heels, avoiding knee lockout. Return slowly, keeping hips and back against the seat.

name: Leg Press Calve Raises
instructions: On a leg press machine, place the balls of your feet on the lower edge. Press toes away, flexing ankles, then return by letting heels drop slightly.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit with back against the pad, handles at shoulder level. Press upward, without locking elbows. Lower with control.

name: EZ Bar Front Raise
instructions: In a standing position hold the EZ bar in the negative position close to your thighs, lower the bar to return to the initial position. Lift the EZ bar in upwards with your arms straight forward and perpendicular to the body and the EZ bar is slightly above shoulder height then lower the EZ bar to the original position.

name: Push Ups
instructions: Hands just outside shoulder-width, body straight. Lower chest near the floor, then press up. Works chest, shoulders, arms, and core stability.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Position a cable at the lowest position possible and attach a single handle. Reach across your body and grab the handle with a neutral grip. Keep the elbow slightly bent and pull the handle across your body and raise laterally. Slowly lower the handle back to the starting

name: Overhead Dumbbell Extension
instructions: Stand or sit while holding a dumbbell with both hands overhead. Lower the weight behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your arms to lift it back up.

name: Plank
instructions: Forearms on the floor, elbows under shoulders. Keep body straight from head to heels, bracing abs and glutes. Avoid hips dropping or rising.

name: Plank Shoulder Taps
instructions: Start in a high plank, feet hip-width. Tap one shoulder with the opposite hand, alternating sides. Minimize torso sway by bracing abs and glutes.

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench and rest your arms on the pad. Curl the weight upward, squeeze your biceps, then lower it back down slowly.

name: Pull Ups
instructions: Grab the handles and pull your chin above the bar and repeat

name: Incline Pull Machine
instructions: Pull handles down until elbows are by your sides and your hands are approximately level with your shoulders

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Squeeze glutes to lift hips into a straight line from shoulders to knees, then lower slowly.

name: Rest
instructions: Rest day

name: Reverse Fly
instructions: Hinge at hips with a flat back, dumbbells below shoulders. Raise arms out to sides, squeezing rear delts and upper back. Keep elbows soft and core tight.

name: Reverse Pec 12k Fly
instructions: Sit at a pec 12k machine facing the pads. With a slight bend in your elbows, pull the pads outward, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then return slowly.

name: Reverse Plank
instructions: Sit with legs straight, hands behind hips. Lift hips to form a straight line from shoulders to heels. Engage glutes and core. Keep shoulders away from ears.

name: Romanian Deadlift Dumbbells
instructions: Stand with dumbbells in front. Keep shoulders back, core tight, slight knee bend. Hinge at hips, lowering weights near shins. Squeeze hamstrings/glutes to return upright.

name: Hanging Leg Raises
instructions: Start by grasping the bar tightly, with your palms over the bar instead of holding on with your fingers. Don't jump up and adjust your hands; if you need, step up from a box to make sure you get a good grip.﻿Lift your legs off the floor and pull your shoulders down, squeezing to engage your upper back. Turn the pits of your elbows forward to activate your lats. Squeeze your abs and glutes. Your legs should be slightly in front of your torso.﻿Bend your knees slightly, then curl your legs up. Think about showing your butt to someone standing in front of you.﻿ Lower back down to the starting position.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit on the bench with your knees bent, grip handle, brace core. Pull handle to your stomach by squeezing shoulder blades. Return with control, maintaining upright posture.

name: Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit or stand holding dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward. Press overhead until arms are nearly locked, then lower under control. Hits front/side delts and triceps.

name: Shrugs
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at sides. Lift shoulders straight up to ears, pause, then lower slowly. Emphasizes upper traps. Keep neck relaxed.

name: Single Arm Overhand Extension
instructions: Stand with one dumbbell overhead, palm facing forward. Keep elbow close to head, bend only at the elbow to lower weight behind neck, then extend upward. Targets the long head of the triceps.

name: Single Leg Deadlift
instructions: Stand on one leg, hinge at hips while extending the other leg behind you. Keep back flat, core braced. Return upright. Enhances balance and hits hamstrings/glutes.

name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Lower into a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, keeping your chest up. Go as deep ws comfortable, ideally until thighs are parallel to the ground. Push through your heels to stand back up, keeping the weight close to your body. Focus on good posture, knee alignment, and controlled movement for the best results.

name: Step Ups
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy step or platform. Step one foot fully onto the step and press through the heel to lift your body up, then step back down. Keep your torso upright and core tight.

name: Superman Hold
instructions: Lie face-down with arms extended. Lift arms, chest, and legs simultaneously, squeezing lower back and glutes. Hold briefly, then lower slowly.

name: Towel Row
instructions: Secure a towel around a pole or handle. Lean back holding both ends. Pull your body forward by driving elbows back, then extend arms to return. Keep core tight, back neutral.

name: Tricep Dips
instructions: Use a bench or chair behind you. Hands on the edge, legs extended or bent. Lower hips by bending elbows, then press back up, focusing on triceps. Keep shoulders away from ears.

name: Tricep Pushdown (Rope)
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with a rope attachment. With elbows pinned to your sides, push the rope downward until your arms are fully extended, then return slowly.
muscleGroups: Long Head Triceps;Lateral Head Triceps;Medial Head Triceps

name: Good Morning
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder - width apart, bent your knees slightly take a barbell on your trapezius muscles of your upper back. Brace your upper back and abdominals and breath in. Exhale as you hinge at the hips, sending the hips backward and upper body forward. Stop Once your upper body is nearly parallel with the floor. Inhale as you return to the starting position.

name: Walking Lunges
instructions: Step forward and lower into a lunge, push off the front foot to move forward. Alternate legs with each step, keeping torso upright and core engaged.

name: Walking Lunges with Dumbbells
instructions: Hold dumbbells at your sides. Step forward into a lunge until both knees form 90° angles. Push through the front heel to stand and bring the rear foot forward to repeat.

name: Wide-Grip Lat Pull-Down
instructions: Sit at a lat pull-down station. Grip the bar wide, pull down toward your upper chest. Focus on squeezing the lats and controlling the bar upward.

name: Wide Push Up
instructions: Hands placed wider than shoulder-width. Keep body rigid, core braced. Lower chest toward the floor, then push back up. Targets outer chest, shoulders, arms.

name: Kneeling Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Position yourself on your knees with your feet crossed. Hold the ab wheel roller directly under your shoulders with your arms fully extended and head facing the ground, not looking forward. Inhale and push the ab roller forward by raising your arms and leaning forward.

name: Reverse Fly
instructions: Raise both arms out to your side on an exhale. Keep soft bend in your elbows. Squeeze the shoulder blades together as you pull them toward the spine, lower the weight back to the start position as you inhale. Avoid hunching your shoulders and keep your chin tucked to maintain neutral spine during the exercise
